component_identification.content: Components include player estate boards, central depot boards with hex tiles by color, goods tiles, worker tokens, silverlings, dice, and score track. Tile colors represent building types: castles, ships, animals, mines, monasteries/knowledge, and various building categories with unique powers. Multiple phase boards organize tile availability over five game phases.

core_game_loop.content: Each turn, roll two dice and take two actions, one per die (modifiable with workers). Typical actions are taking a tile from depot, placing a tile into your estate, selling goods, or taking worker tokens. Dice create tactical constraints while tile powers and estate planning drive long-term optimization.

detailed_rules.content: Tile placement requires matching die value to both source depot and destination estate space number unless modified by workers. Placed tiles often trigger immediate effects, including extra actions, goods, points, money, or tile manipulation. Completing regions of same-color spaces awards points based on phase timing and region size, rewarding early completion bonuses. Ships improve turn order and provide goods, mines grant recurring silverlings, castles offer flexible bonus actions, and yellow knowledge tiles alter rules/scoring. Buildings (beige) have subtype powers that can chain with other actions. Goods are sold using die values matching storage slots, giving points and silverlings. Balancing short-term die efficiency with region-completion timing is the core tension.

scoring_and_endgame.content: After five phases, players score completed regions, sold goods, some yellow tile bonuses, and endgame effects. Unused workers/silverlings may grant small points depending edition rules. Highest VP wins; ties usually favor player with more unused resources or shared victory per ruleset.

beginner_strategy.content: Collect workers early to smooth bad dice turns. Prioritize ships and mines in opening phases for turn-order control and economy. Complete small regions quickly for timing bonuses instead of delaying for perfect layouts. Treat yellow knowledge tiles as strategic commitments—take ones that fit your plan, not just availability. Keep goods cycling so storage does not clog and miss scoring chances.
